update.title: Two steps
update.lede: Upload firmware and the UI image. The device reboots automatically after each step. Update firmware first if the new UI depends on it.
update.step1.title: Firmware (app)
update.step1.help: Upload the firmware (firmware.bin). The device will reboot automatically after a successful upload.
update.step2.title: UI (LittleFS)
update.step2.help: Upload the UI image (littlefs.bin). The device will reboot automatically after this step as well.
update.step2.note: Updates are applied immediately per step. After reboot you'll return to the dashboard.
update.versions.title: Versions
update.versions.label: Current
logs.title: Archive
logs.lede: Download archived log files. Newest entries first.
logs.section.title: Available files
logs.section.label: Logs
mqtt.title: Broker
mqtt.lede: Configure the MQTT connection and Home Assistant discovery. The clock reconnects immediately after saving.
mqtt.status.title: Status
mqtt.status.label: Link
mqtt.broker.title: Broker
mqtt.broker.label: Server
mqtt.broker.host_help: Enter the broker the clock should connect to.
mqtt.broker.auth_help: User + password are required, unless you explicitly opt for no authentication.
mqtt.ha.title: Home Assistant
mqtt.ha.label: Topics
mqtt.ha.basetopic_help: Used as a prefix for all states and commands.
mqtt.save.title: Save
mqtt.save.label: Apply
mqtt.save.help: Change settings and save. The clock will try to reconnect immediately.
mqtt.clear.confirm: Clear MQTT settings and disable MQTT?
mqtt.err.user_required: User is required unless "no authentication" is checked.
mqtt.err.pass_required: Password is required unless "no authentication" is checked.
admin.title: Service
admin.lede: Production and service actions. This page is protected by the manufacturer password — critical endpoints are not accessible without it.

admin.channel.label: Choose
admin.channel.develop: Develop — may render the clock unusable; recovery not guaranteed
admin.channel.default_help: Default: stable. Early/Develop may be unstable.
admin.channel.develop_confirm: Use the develop channel? This may render the clock unusable; recovery is not guaranteed.
admin.sync.title: UI sync
admin.sync.label: Manifest
admin.sync.confirm: Sync UI files from manifest? This may take a moment.
admin.sync.triggered: UI sync triggered. Check logs for progress.
admin.logs.title: Log settings
admin.logs.label: Retention
admin.logs.retention_help: Number of days to keep log files (1–10). Default is 1.

admin.fleet.help: Register this device with fleet management.

admin.danger.title: Danger
admin.danger.label: Reset
admin.danger.help: Erases stored settings. The device will reboot to a fresh state.
admin.danger.confirm: Are you sure you want to perform a full factory reset?
admin.danger.started: Factory reset started. Device will reboot shortly…
admin.bootstrap.title: Re-install bootstrap
admin.bootstrap.label: Provisioning
admin.bootstrap.help: Replaces the running firmware with the bootstrap provisioning image (stable channel). After reboot, this device hosts the first-flash product picker at http://wordclock.local/ and stops acting as a clock until a product is re-installed.
admin.bootstrap.confirm:
Replace the current firmware with the bootstrap provisioning image?

After reboot, this device stops being a clock and hosts the first-flash product picker at http://wordclock.local/. You will need to re-pick a product to bring the clock back.
admin.bootstrap.waiting: Install started. Waiting for the device to reboot into bootstrap…
admin.bootstrap.in_progress: Install in progress — device still online…
admin.bootstrap.offline: Device offline — OTA running / device rebooting…
admin.bootstrap.timeout: Bootstrap did not come back within 3 minutes. Try opening http://wordclock.local/ manually, or power-cycle the device.
admin.ota.confirm: Check for firmware updates and start OTA?
admin.ota.started: Update started. Device will reboot shortly…
